arabic: وَحَدَّثَنِي عَنْ مَالِكٍ، عَنِ ابْنِ شِهَابٍ، أَنَّهُ قَالَ مَنْ أَهْدَى بَدَنَةً جَزَاءً أَوْ نَذْرًا أَوْ هَدْىَ تَمَتُّعٍ فَأُصِيبَتْ فِي الطَّرِيقِ فَعَلَيْهِ الْبَدَلُ ‏.‏

english.text:
Yahya related to me from Malik that Ibn Shihab said, "If someone
dedicates an animal as compensation, or for a vow, or as the sacrifice
for tamattu, and misfortune befalls it on the road, he must provide a
substitute."
